pattern: A best path bends at its highest node; track the best single-sided gain each subtree can hand upward while separately recording the best full bend seen anywhere.
hints: A path may start and end anywhere, so the optimum can live entirely inside a subtree and never touch the root.
From any node, what you can pass to your parent is the node's value plus the larger of its two child gains (and never a negative gain — drop it to zero).
While computing that upward gain, also consider the path that bends at this node (value + max(0,left) + max(0,right)) as a candidate for the global answer.

edge_inputs.description: bend at the root through both children
negative root is skipped; best path bends below it
single node, the answer is that value
single negative node — must still pick it, can't pick empty
all negatives — answer is the least-bad single node
long positive chain, straight path wins

stress.description: random tree mixing positives and negatives
